[2018-06-29 18:27] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): Doesn't bind violate SDR, because now units with the option to click are sometimes worse than units that must attack.
[2018-06-29 18:31] namington: **Savior's Savior's Savior** 12 :Gold: (1 supply)
4 :Time:, 4 :Lifespan:, 4 :Health: 
Additional Cost: Consume a Savior 
Start of Turn: Construct a Savior's Savior
[2018-06-29 18:33] xujhan: I don't think that violates SDR. My understanding is that you only need to prefer having the unit to not having the unit.
[2018-06-29 18:33] namington: Literally unbuyable in most sets
[2018-06-29 18:34] namington: Nah it does violate sdr
[2018-06-29 18:34] namington: Currently 10 steelsplitters is objectively better than 10 tarsiers
[2018-06-29 18:34] namington: But bind makes that not the case
[2018-06-29 18:34] namington: SO if you take "more versatile" as a trait that matters for sdr
[2018-06-29 18:35] namington: I guess ampo violates SDR by that definition
[2018-06-29 18:36] namington: So maybe I'm wrong :ThinkTank:
[2018-06-29 18:37] namington: It DOES mean that a hypothetical drone that cannot be unclicked is no longer dominated by a drone that can be
[2018-06-29 18:37] namington: But that currently doesn't exist so
[2018-06-29 18:37] namington: Wait that doesn't work on mobile wtf
[2018-06-29 18:37] supergreyman (Greyman): I think of this in a situation where your opponent's Wincer is at 1 health. It would be better to buy a measly Militia than buying Omega.
[2018-06-29 18:39] supergreyman (Greyman): Probably not a good unit for use in this case since Militia has a different click ability....
[2018-06-29 18:43] namington: Idk that "militia is better than omega" violates SDR
[2018-06-29 18:43] namington: A position with omega need not dominate an identical position with militia
[2018-06-29 18:44] namington: (although it is obviously generally better)
[2018-06-29 18:45] xujhan: There are already "beneficial" traits that aren't always beneficial (prompt, golden, etc).
[2018-06-29 18:46] xujhan: I don't see why "clickable" would be treated any differently.
[2018-06-29 18:56] supergreyman (Greyman): They are added because even though there are situations you don't want certain units to be prompt or golden, the overall positive effect they create heavily outweighs the negative.

It could be the same with the binding effect, but I'm not sure if it creates more interesting game without testing.

[2018-07-03 01:01] kaelii: Ampo can be 3 health mostly because tarsiers are double breachvulnerable per ampo. If your choice is kill 3 tarsiers or kill ampo the choice is pretty frequently the tarsiers anyway.
[2018-07-03 01:02] kaelii: If I have 2 ampos my tarsiers are triple breach vulnerable and killing an ampo is basically NEVER correct, because 3 health in tarsiers is 9 damage vs a typical maximum of 10 on ampo.
[2018-07-03 01:03] kaelii: The exception is for apollo where the snipe still kills the ampo.
